questions.subject: Environment
questions.prompt:
Consider the following statements regarding the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP):
1. It covers all urban local bodies across India to comprehensively tackle air pollution.
2. It targets up to a 40% reduction in PM10 levels by 2025-26 from the 2019-20 base levels.
3. The PRANA portal has been launched to monitor its implementation.
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: NCAP does not cover all urban local bodies; it specifically covers 131 non-attainment and Million Plus Cities across 24 States/UTs. Statement 2 is correct: It targets up to a 40% reduction in PM10 levels by 2025-26 from 2019-20 levels. Statement 3 is correct: The implementation of NCAP is monitored via the PRANA portal.
questions.takeaway: NCAP is a targeted program for 131 non-attainment and Million Plus Cities, aiming for a 40% reduction in PM10 by 2025-26 (base year 2019-20), monitored through the PRANA portal.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: NCAP targets a reduction in PM10 level up to 40%... by 2025-26 from 2019-20 levels. Implemented by CPCB and monitored via the PRANA portal. NCAP covers 131 non-attainment and Million Plus Cities across 24 States/UTs.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.prompt:
With reference to the Swachh Vayu Sarveshan Awards, consider the following statements:
1. They are announced annually by the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA) as part of urban sanitation initiatives.
2. The awards assess and rank only those cities that are covered under the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP).
3. In the 2025 awards, Indore secured the first rank in the category of cities with a population of less than 3 lakhs.
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The awards are announced by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC), not MoHUA. Statement 2 is correct: The awards recognize the best-performing cities among the 130 NCAP cities. Statement 3 is incorrect: Indore won in Category 1 (population >10 lakh), while Dewas won in Category 3 (population <3 lakh).
questions.takeaway: The Swachh Vayu Sarveshan Awards are an initiative of the MoEFCC to rank the 130 NCAP cities based on their air quality improvement efforts. Indore topped the >10 lakh population category in 2025.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Awards announced by MoEFCC recognizing best-performing cities among 130 NCAP cities. Category 1 (>10 lakh) won by Indore; Category 2 (3-10 lakh) by Amravati; Category 3 (<3 lakh) by Dewas.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.prompt:
Consider the following statements regarding the institutional framework and targets of the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP):
1. The Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) is the implementing agency for NCAP.
2. Under NCAP, the target for the national ambient air quality standard for PM10 is set at 60 micrograms per cubic meter.
3. The base year for measuring the targeted reduction in particulate matter concentration under NCAP is 2014-15.
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: NCAP is implemented by the CPCB. Statement 2 is correct: The program targets achieving national ambient air quality standards of 60 microgram/m3 for PM10. Statement 3 is incorrect: The base year for the targeted reduction (up to 40% by 2025-26) is 2019-20, not 2014-15.
questions.takeaway: NCAP, implemented by the CPCB, uses 2019-20 as the base year to achieve up to a 40% reduction in PM10 levels or to reach the national standard of 60 microgram/m3 by 2025-26.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: NCAP (launched 2019) targets a reduction in PM10 level up to 40% or achievement of national ambient air quality standards (60 microgram/m3) by 2025-26 from 2019-20 levels. Implemented by CPCB

questions.subject: Environment
questions.prompt: With reference to the implementation of the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) and the Swachh Vayu Sarveshan, which of the following statements most accurately reflects their operational design?

options.text: The programme mandates a uniform absolute reduction in particulate matter for all cities, categorizing them by geographical area for performance tracking.
The programme targets either a percentage reduction in PM10 levels or the achievement of a specific concentration threshold, with cities competing for awards within population-based categories.
The programme focuses exclusively on million-plus cities, evaluating their performance through the PRANA portal based on the number of air quality monitoring stations installed.
The programme requires cities to achieve a 40% reduction in both PM2.5 and PM10 levels, categorizing them for awards based on their baseline pollution levels in 2019-20.

questions.explanation: NCAP provides a dual compliance pathway: cities can either achieve up to a 40% reduction in PM10 levels from the 2019-20 baseline or reach the national ambient air quality standard of 60 micrograms per cubic meter. Furthermore, the Swachh Vayu Sarveshan categorizes these cities based on population (e.g., >10 lakh, 3-10 lakh, <3 lakh) rather than geographical area or baseline pollution levels.
questions.takeaway: NCAP's flexible target design allows cities to comply by either reducing PM10 by 40% or meeting the 60 µg/m³ standard. Swachh Vayu Sarveshan ensures fair competition by grouping cities by population size.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Category 1 (>10 lakh) won by Indore; Category 2 (3-10 lakh) by Amravati; Category 3 (<3 lakh) by Dewas. NCAP (launched 2019) targets a reduction in PM10 level up to 40% or achievement of national ambient air quality standards (60 microgram/m3) by 2025-26 from 2019-20 levels.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.prompt:
Consider the following pairs of cities and their respective winning categories under the Swachh Vayu Sarveshan Award 2025:

1. Indore : Population greater than 10 lakhs
2. Amravati : Population between 3 to 10 lakhs
3. Dewas : Population less than 3 lakhs

How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?
questions.explanation: All three pairs are correctly matched. The Swachh Vayu Sarveshan Award 2025 recognized Indore as the winner in Category 1 (>10 lakh population), Amravati in Category 2 (3-10 lakh population), and Dewas in Category 3 (<3 lakh population).
questions.takeaway: Swachh Vayu Sarveshan categorizes cities into three population brackets to evaluate air quality improvements, with Indore, Amravati, and Dewas winning their respective categories in 2025.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Category 1 (>10 lakh) won by Indore; Category 2 (3-10 lakh) by Amravati; Category 3 (<3 lakh) by Dewas.
